name: Cut-Out Sugar Cookies
recipeInstructions:
Preparation Time: 15 Minutes
Chill Time: 2 hours
Bake Time: 7-9 Minutes
1. Cream the Butter Flavor Crisco, sugar, milk (1 tablespoon plus 1 teaspoon), and vanilla extract in a large bowl at medium speed of an electric mixer until well blended. Beat in the egg.
2. Mix together the flour, baking powder, and salt. Gradually incorporate this mixture into the creamed ingredients. Cover and refrigerate for several hours or overnight.
3. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
4. Roll out half of the dough at a time on a floured surface until it is about 1/8 inch thick. Cut into desired shapes using cookie cutters, placing them about 2 inches apart on an ungreased baking sheet. Sprinkle with colored sugar and decorations if desired.
5. Bake for 7 to 9 minutes in the preheated oven. Allow cookies to cool slightly before transferring them to a cooling rack.
Makes approximately 3 dozen cookies (depending on size and shape).
Variations:
- Lemon or Orange Cookie Variation: Add 1 teaspoon grated lemon or orange peel and 1 teaspoon lemon or orange extract during step 1.
- Creamy Vanilla Frosting: Combine the Butter Flavor Crisco, confectioners' sugar (4 cups), milk (1/3 cup), and vanilla extract in a medium bowl at low speed of an electric mixer until well blended. Scrape the bowl and beat at high speed for 2 minutes or until smooth and creamy. Frost cooled cookies.
For coloring, use 1-2 drops of food color per cup of frosting if desired.
Lemon or Orange Frosting Variation: Eliminate milk from step 3 and add lemon or orange juice (1/3 cup) with the orange peel during preparation.
